decisions.summary: Q4 resolved: dialog checkboxes default to ALL conversations selected, with Select-all / Select-none controls. Matches the issue author's stated default and 'carry all over' intent; trivially reversible UI default, not escalated.

Add a purpose-built NON-destructive write-door function importLegacyConversation (+ setImportedConversationLinks) to src/lib/overdeck/conversations.ts. Do NOT route imports through createConversation (conversations.ts:1153) — it DELETEs same-name rows and hardcodes status='active'/archived_at=NULL, dropping created_at/cost/tokens/ended/FK columns (violates AC5 + the mapping). Do NOT use ConversationWriter.create (415) — it cannot pass claudeSessionId (AC6). Extending the writer keeps the single-source-of-truth tenet; routes never write the tables directly.

Add readOnly?: boolean to OpenDatabaseOptions in src/lib/database/driver.ts. openDatabase already forwards the whole options object to new DatabaseSync(path, options) and node:sqlite honors readOnly, so the type addition is sufficient for the Node-only dashboard server. The bun branch (driver.ts:288) is not exercised here.

All import-specific logic lives in ONE deletable module src/lib/overdeck/legacy-import.ts (preview + two-pass import + favorites). The whole legacy-import path (incl. getLegacyHome()) is slated for removal at cutover per src/lib/paths.ts:14-18 — keep it isolated so the future delete is clean.

Two-pass insert: pass 1 inserts all selected conversations with FK columns NULL (minting UUIDs, building a {legacyRowid → newUuid} map); pass 2 sets handoff_target_conv_id/cleared_to_conv_id from the map (NULL + per-row warning when the target was not imported). Avoids FK-constraint violations regardless of PRAGMA foreign_keys.

Idempotency: skip-and-report (never DELETE, never overwrite) when a conversation with the same name exists, or when the legacy row's claude_session_id matches an existing conversation_files.locator via getConversationByClaudeSessionId (conversations.ts:961). Report in skipped[].
A legacy row with empty/unparseable created_at fails that single row (reason in failed[]); never insert a zero timestamp. created_at is NOT NULL in both legacy and new schemas. Other rows proceed.

status is copied through (null → derive 'ended' if archived_at else 'active'). Imported conversations have no live tmux session, so the liveness oracle reconciles any 'active' import to 'ended' on its next poll — no special-casing.

hazards.summary: Sacred-JSONL invariant: the importer must never move, copy, delete, or mutate any ~/.claude/projects/**/*.jsonl backing transcript file (AC6).
hazards.mitigation: Copy DB metadata only; carry claude_session_id into conversation_files so the existing transcript reader resolves the file in place. No filesystem writes to transcript paths.

hazards.summary: Re-pointing the singleton overdeck DB handle at the legacy DB would corrupt the live connection.
hazards.mitigation: legacy-import.ts opens its OWN read-only handle via openDatabase(path,{readOnly:true}) and closes it; it never calls getDatabase()/getOverdeckDatabaseSync() for the source. (NFR-1 / AC8.)

hazards.summary: The pre-rebrand ~/.panopticon/panopticon.db will NOT exist on the dev machine at implementation time, so tests cannot read a live source DB.
hazards.mitigation: Tests build a fixture legacy DB from the legacy conversations DDL (src/lib/database/schema.ts:471) + a legacy favorites row, and the ?path= param points preview/import at the fixture.

hazards.summary: createConversation's destructive DELETE-then-insert (conversations.ts:1173-1174) would silently destroy a same-named overdeck conversation if reused for import.
hazards.mitigation: Use the dedicated non-destructive importLegacyConversation (D2); a test asserts no DELETE is issued against the overdeck conversations table (AC5 / legacy-import-write.ac3).

hazards.summary: If PRAGMA foreign_keys is ON, a one-pass insert with a forward FK reference (handoff_target_conv_id/cleared_to_conv_id) throws.
hazards.mitigation: Two-pass insert (D5): all rows inserted with NULL FKs first, then a second pass sets the remapped UUIDs.

hazards.summary: The POST import endpoint mutates overdeck.db and must be CSRF-guarded.
hazards.mitigation: Call rejectUnsafeDashboardMutationRequest(request) before importing (NFR-2); the frontend sends dashboardMutationJsonHeaders().

sessionHistory.note: Planning role authored the vBRIEF directly after verifying every issue reference against the tree at SHA a1d01b2b9 and resolving the two under-specified design gaps (lossy/destructive write doors; missing read-only driver option) plus Q4.

pipeline.reviewNotes: POST import can import agent/planning/specialist legacy conversations despite the exclusion requirement
pipeline.verificationNotes:
Acceptance criteria check FAILED — 20/20 AC incomplete:

### Add a read-only open option to the shared SQLite driver (2/2 incomplete)
  - [ ] OpenDatabaseOptions in src/lib/database/driver.ts contains an optional readOnly boolean field.
  - [ ] A unit test calls openDatabase(fixturePath, { readOnly: true }), asserts a SELECT succeeds, and asserts a write statement (INSERT or CREATE TABLE) against the handle throws.

### Read-only legacy-conversation preview module (4/4 incomplete)
  - [ ] src/lib/overdeck/legacy-import.ts exports previewLegacyConversations(path) that opens the legacy DB via openDatabase(path, { readOnly: true }) on its own handle and never calls getDatabase()/getOverdeckDatabaseSync().
  - [ ] Given a fixture legacy DB seeded from the legacy conversations DDL, previewLegacyConversations returns one row per non-agent conversation and excludes every name matched by isAgentConversationName (agent-/planning-/specialist-).
  - [ ] Each preview row includes name, title, createdAt, model, hasFavorite (true when a legacy favorites row of type 'conversation' matches the name), and alreadyImported (true when a same-name conversation already exists in overdeck.db).
  - [ ] When path points at a non-existent file, the module returns a not-found result without throwing.

### Non-destructive import write door + two-pass orchestration + favorites (6/6 incomplete)
  - [ ] conversations.ts exports a non-destructive importLegacyConversation(mapped) that inserts without deleting any existing row and registers a conversation_files locator when claudeSessionId is present; a fixture test asserts created_at (ISO→ms), archived_at, status, ended_at, total_cost, total_tokens, title, title_source round-trip to the new row and the conversation_files locator equals the legacy claude_session_id.
  - [ ] importLegacyConversations skips and reports (in skipped[]) any selected name already present in overdeck.db (matched by name, or by claude_session_id via getConversationByClaudeSessionId) — no duplicate row, no overwrite; a test pre-creates a same-named conversation and asserts it is unchanged after import.
  - [ ] The import path preserves existing overdeck rows: it issues no DELETE against the conversations table and does not route through createConversation (verified by a test and/or a grep over the import module).
  - [ ] handoff_target_conv_id and cleared_to_conv_id are remapped from legacy rowids to the imported targets' new UUIDs when those targets are also imported, and set NULL with a per-row warning when the target was not imported — a fixture test covers both branches.
  - [ ] A legacy row with an empty or unparseable created_at fails that row with a recorded reason in failed[], and no zero-timestamp row is inserted into overdeck.db.
  - [ ] For each imported conversation whose name matches a legacy type='conversation' favorite, the import creates a favorite via setFavorite and favoritesCarried records the count; legacy favorites of other types are ignored — fixture test.

### Settings endpoints: legacy-import preview (GET) and import (POST) (4/4 incomplete)
  - [ ] GET /api/settings/legacy-import/conversations returns { found:false, defaultPath, message } when no DB exists at the resolved path and { found:true, path, conversations } (via previewLegacyConversations) when one does; ?path= overrides the default join(getLegacyHome(),'panopticon.db').
  - [ ] POST /api/settings/legacy-import/conversations with body { path, names } calls importLegacyConversations and returns { imported, skipped, failed, warnings, favoritesCarried }.
  - [ ] The POST route calls rejectUnsafeDashboardMutationRequest(request) and returns its auth-error response when the dashboard CSRF token is absent, before any import runs.
  - [ ] Both routes are added to settingsRouteLayer in settings.ts and a route-level test exercises the GET preview against a fixture path and the POST import summary.

### Experimental settings button + LegacyImportDialog (4/4 incomplete)
  - [ ] A button labeled 'Import conversations from old Panopticon' renders in the Experimental section of SettingsPage.tsx and opens LegacyImportDialog (state legacyImportOpen).
  - [ ] The dialog renders preview rows from the GET endpoint with per-row checkboxes that default to all-selected, plus Select-all / Select-none controls; alreadyImported rows render disabled with an 'already imported' tag.
  - [ ] The 'Import N conversations' button POSTs the selected names with dashboardMutationJsonHeaders() and, on success, shows a summary of imported / skipped / failed (with reasons) / favorites carried plus a link to the Conversations panel.
  - [ ] When the GET response is { found:false }, the dialog shows 'No legacy database found at <path>' with a manual path input that re-runs the preview.
pipeline.inspectNotes: Spec fidelity: importLegacyConversation and setImportedConversationLinks correctly added to conversations.ts (non-destructive INSERT OR IGNORE, randomUUID, full column mapping, conversation_files locator on claudeSessionId). importLegacyConversations in legacy-import.ts implements two-pass orchestration: pass 1 skips-by-name and skips-by-claude_session_id then inserts; pass 2 remaps FK rowids to UUIDs with NULL+warning for unresolved targets. Favorites carry-over via setFavorite. failed[] for unparseable created_at. No DELETE in import path. All 6 ACs covered by fixture tests.
